plssem 0.1.1
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Categorical Models with plssem
    • Higher Order Models
    • Interaction Models with plssem
    • Linear Models with plssem
    • Handling Missing Values with plssem
    • Multilevel Models with plssem
    • Ordinal Regression with plssem
    • Parallel Bootstrapping with plssem

Multilevel Models with plssem

Source: vignettes/multilevel-models.Rmd
multilevel-models.Rmd

This vignette shows examples of multilevel random slopes and intercept models, with both continuous and ordinal data.

Random Slopes Model 

slopes_model <- "
  X =~ x1 + x2 + x3
  Z =~ z1 + z2 + z3
  Y =~ y1 + y2 + y3
  W =~ w1 + w2 + w3
  Y ~ X + Z + (1 + X + Z | cluster)
  W ~ X + Z + (1 + X + Z | cluster)
"

Continuous Indicators 

fit_slopes_cont <- pls(
  slopes_model,
  data      = randomSlopes,
  bootstrap = TRUE,
  boot.R    = 50
)
summary(fit_slopes_cont)
#> plssem (0.1.1) ended normally after 3 iterations
#> 
#>   Estimator                                   PLSc-MLM
#>   Link                                          PROBIT
#>                                                       
#>   Number of observations                          5000
#>   Number of iterations                               3
#>   Number of latent variables                         4
#>   Number of observed variables                      18
#> 
#> Fit Measures:
#>   Chi-Square                                   127.210
#>   Degrees of Freedom                                49
#>   SRMR                                           0.008
#>   RMSEA                                          0.018
#> 
#> R-squared (indicators):
#>   x1                                             0.860
#>   x2                                             0.683
#>   x3                                             0.772
#>   z1                                             0.839
#>   z2                                             0.694
#>   z3                                             0.759
#>   y1                                             0.838
#>   y2                                             0.726
#>   y3                                             0.750
#>   w1                                             0.840
#>   w2                                             0.695
#>   w3                                             0.771
#> 
#> R-squared (latents):
#>   Y                                              0.542
#>   W                                              0.553
#> 
#> Latent Variables:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   X =~          
#>     x1              0.927      0.009  103.575    0.000
#>     x2              0.826      0.011   75.208    0.000
#>     x3              0.879      0.007  118.911    0.000
#>   Z =~          
#>     z1              0.916      0.010   92.652    0.000
#>     z2              0.833      0.013   66.169    0.000
#>     z3              0.871      0.009   93.271    0.000
#>   Y =~          
#>     y1              0.915      0.010   87.406    0.000
#>     y2              0.852      0.013   64.451    0.000
#>     y3              0.866      0.014   60.663    0.000
#>   W =~          
#>     w1              0.916      0.011   84.877    0.000
#>     w2              0.834      0.012   68.770    0.000
#>     w3              0.878      0.015   58.239    0.000
#> 
#> Regressions:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   Y ~           
#>     X               0.293      0.018   16.673    0.000
#>     Z               0.444      0.033   13.283    0.000
#>   W ~           
#>     X               0.393      0.033   12.011    0.000
#>     Z               0.248      0.057    4.361    0.000
#> 
#> Intercepts:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .Y               0.010      0.004    2.400    0.016
#>    .W               0.010      0.009    1.098    0.272
#> 
#> Covariances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   X ~~          
#>     Z               0.175      0.013   13.057    0.000
#>   Y~X ~~        
#>     Y~1            -0.004      0.006   -0.641    0.522
#>   Y~Z ~~        
#>     Y~1            -0.025      0.013   -1.981    0.048
#>     Y~X             0.012      0.009    1.445    0.148
#>   W~X ~~        
#>     W~1             0.003      0.009    0.335    0.738
#>   W~Z ~~        
#>     W~1             0.009      0.012    0.762    0.446
#>     W~X             0.013      0.013    1.035    0.301
#> 
#> Variances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>     X               1.000                             
#>     Z               1.000                             
#>    .Y               0.458      0.026   17.853    0.000
#>    .W               0.447      0.031   14.200    0.000
#>    .x1              0.140      0.017    8.427    0.000
#>    .x2              0.317      0.018   17.444    0.000
#>    .x3              0.228      0.013   17.505    0.000
#>    .z1              0.161      0.018    8.905    0.000
#>    .z2              0.306      0.021   14.560    0.000
#>    .z3              0.241      0.016   14.768    0.000
#>    .y1              0.162      0.019    8.473    0.000
#>    .y2              0.274      0.022   12.241    0.000
#>    .y3              0.250      0.025   10.103    0.000
#>    .w1              0.160      0.020    8.065    0.000
#>    .w2              0.305      0.020   15.149    0.000
#>    .w3              0.229      0.026    8.644    0.000
#>     Y~1             0.086      0.023    3.790    0.000
#>     Y~X             0.018      0.005    3.938    0.000
#>     Y~Z             0.105      0.013    7.841    0.000
#>     W~1             0.059      0.013    4.607    0.000
#>     W~X             0.095      0.017    5.554    0.000
#>     W~Z             0.144      0.029    5.008    0.000

Ordered Indicators 

fit_slopes_ord <- pls(
  slopes_model,
  data      = randomSlopesOrdered,
  bootstrap = TRUE,
  boot.R    = 50,
  ordered   = colnames(randomSlopesOrdered) # explicitly specify variables as ordered
)
summary(fit_slopes_ord)
#> plssem (0.1.1) ended normally after 3 iterations
#> 
#>   Estimator                                OrdPLSc-MLM
#>   Link                                          PROBIT
#>                                                       
#>   Number of observations                          5000
#>   Number of iterations                               3
#>   Number of latent variables                         4
#>   Number of observed variables                      18
#> 
#> Fit Measures:
#>   Chi-Square                                   263.372
#>   Degrees of Freedom                                49
#>   SRMR                                           0.010
#>   RMSEA                                          0.030
#> 
#> R-squared (indicators):
#>   x1                                             0.870
#>   x2                                             0.669
#>   x3                                             0.789
#>   z1                                             0.841
#>   z2                                             0.714
#>   z3                                             0.758
#>   y1                                             0.844
#>   y2                                             0.711
#>   y3                                             0.754
#>   w1                                             0.835
#>   w2                                             0.681
#>   w3                                             0.785
#> 
#> R-squared (latents):
#>   Y                                              0.539
#>   W                                              0.550
#> 
#> Latent Variables:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   X =~          
#>     x1              0.933      0.015   61.550    0.000
#>     x2              0.818      0.011   71.661    0.000
#>     x3              0.888      0.013   66.617    0.000
#>   Z =~          
#>     z1              0.917      0.011   83.696    0.000
#>     z2              0.845      0.011   73.930    0.000
#>     z3              0.871      0.012   72.237    0.000
#>   Y =~          
#>     y1              0.918      0.011   85.820    0.000
#>     y2              0.843      0.014   61.187    0.000
#>     y3              0.869      0.013   66.265    0.000
#>   W =~          
#>     w1              0.914      0.012   76.861    0.000
#>     w2              0.825      0.017   48.766    0.000
#>     w3              0.886      0.016   56.212    0.000
#> 
#> Regressions:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   Y ~           
#>     X               0.290      0.026   11.309    0.000
#>     Z               0.451      0.034   13.247    0.000
#>   W ~           
#>     X               0.391      0.032   12.268    0.000
#>     Z               0.245      0.053    4.629    0.000
#> 
#> Intercepts:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .Y               0.011      0.005    2.432    0.015
#>    .W               0.009      0.007    1.325    0.185
#> 
#> Covariances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   X ~~          
#>     Z               0.168      0.012   13.679    0.000
#>   Y~X ~~        
#>     Y~1            -0.007      0.006   -1.074    0.283
#>   Y~Z ~~        
#>     Y~1            -0.025      0.016   -1.552    0.121
#>     Y~X             0.012      0.008    1.599    0.110
#>   W~X ~~        
#>     W~1             0.004      0.010    0.363    0.716
#>   W~Z ~~        
#>     W~1             0.010      0.013    0.747    0.455
#>     W~X             0.016      0.013    1.281    0.200
#> 
#> Variances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>     X               1.000                             
#>     Z               1.000                             
#>    .Y               0.461      0.025   18.469    0.000
#>    .W               0.450      0.031   14.687    0.000
#>    .x1              0.130      0.028    4.620    0.000
#>    .x2              0.331      0.019   17.684    0.000
#>    .x3              0.211      0.024    8.879    0.000
#>    .z1              0.159      0.020    7.944    0.000
#>    .z2              0.286      0.019   14.756    0.000
#>    .z3              0.242      0.021   11.521    0.000
#>    .y1              0.156      0.020    7.989    0.000
#>    .y2              0.289      0.023   12.454    0.000
#>    .y3              0.246      0.023   10.756    0.000
#>    .w1              0.165      0.022    7.566    0.000
#>    .w2              0.319      0.028   11.429    0.000
#>    .w3              0.215      0.028    7.769    0.000
#>     Y~1             0.084      0.019    4.421    0.000
#>     Y~X             0.018      0.005    3.843    0.000
#>     Y~Z             0.101      0.013    8.101    0.000
#>     W~1             0.058      0.015    3.821    0.000
#>     W~X             0.099      0.019    5.346    0.000
#>     W~Z             0.142      0.035    4.057    0.000

Random Intercepts Model 

intercepts_model <- '
  f =~ y1 + y2 + y3
  f ~ x1 + x2 + x3 + w1 + w2 + (1 | cluster)
'

Continuous Indicators 

fit_intercepts_cont <- pls(
  intercepts_model,
  data      = randomIntercepts,
  bootstrap = TRUE,
  boot.R    = 50
)
summary(fit_intercepts_cont)
#> plssem (0.1.1) ended normally after 2 iterations
#> 
#>   Estimator                                   PLSc-MLM
#>   Link                                          PROBIT
#>                                                       
#>   Number of observations                         10000
#>   Number of iterations                               2
#>   Number of latent variables                         1
#>   Number of observed variables                       9
#> 
#> Fit Measures:
#>   Chi-Square                                    24.372
#>   Degrees of Freedom                                10
#>   SRMR                                           0.003
#>   RMSEA                                          0.012
#> 
#> R-squared (indicators):
#>   y1                                             0.891
#>   y2                                             0.785
#>   y3                                             0.814
#> 
#> R-squared (latents):
#>   f                                              0.744
#> 
#> Latent Variables:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   f =~          
#>     y1              0.944      0.009  110.728    0.000
#>     y2              0.886      0.011   82.093    0.000
#>     y3              0.902      0.008  109.560    0.000
#> 
#> Regressions:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   f ~           
#>     x1              0.238      0.007   33.727    0.000
#>     x2              0.162      0.007   23.198    0.000
#>     x3              0.077      0.005   16.079    0.000
#>     w1              0.128      0.044    2.885    0.004
#>     w2              0.091      0.037    2.480    0.013
#> 
#> Intercepts:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .f              -0.013      0.008   -1.597    0.110
#> 
#> Covariances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   x1 ~~         
#>     x2              0.104      0.010   10.336    0.000
#>     x3              0.004      0.011    0.391    0.696
#>     w1              0.000                             
#>     w2              0.000                             
#>   x2 ~~         
#>     x3              0.097      0.010   10.087    0.000
#>     w1              0.000                             
#>     w2              0.000                             
#>   x3 ~~         
#>     w1              0.000                             
#>     w2              0.000                             
#>   w1 ~~         
#>     w2             -0.041      0.040   -1.026    0.305
#> 
#> Variances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .f               0.256      0.015   17.108    0.000
#>     x1              1.000                             
#>     x2              1.000                             
#>     x3              1.000                             
#>     w1              1.000                             
#>     w2              1.000                             
#>    .y1              0.109      0.016    6.736    0.000
#>    .y2              0.215      0.019   11.179    0.000
#>    .y3              0.186      0.015   12.540    0.000
#>     f~1             0.621      0.018   34.316    0.000

Ordered Indicators 

fit_intercepts_ord <- pls(
  intercepts_model,
  data      = randomInterceptsOrdered,
  bootstrap = TRUE,
  boot.R    = 50,
  ordered   = colnames(randomInterceptsOrdered) # explicitly specify variables as ordered
)
summary(fit_intercepts_ord)
#> plssem (0.1.1) ended normally after 2 iterations
#> 
#>   Estimator                                OrdPLSc-MLM
#>   Link                                          PROBIT
#>                                                       
#>   Number of observations                         10000
#>   Number of iterations                               2
#>   Number of latent variables                         1
#>   Number of observed variables                       9
#> 
#> Fit Measures:
#>   Chi-Square                                    25.508
#>   Degrees of Freedom                                10
#>   SRMR                                           0.003
#>   RMSEA                                          0.012
#> 
#> R-squared (indicators):
#>   y1                                             0.885
#>   y2                                             0.788
#>   y3                                             0.809
#> 
#> R-squared (latents):
#>   f                                              0.723
#> 
#> Latent Variables:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   f =~          
#>     y1              0.941      0.011   81.861    0.000
#>     y2              0.888      0.010   91.937    0.000
#>     y3              0.899      0.012   77.555    0.000
#> 
#> Regressions:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   f ~           
#>     x1              0.241      0.009   27.289    0.000
#>     x2              0.158      0.008   19.860    0.000
#>     x3              0.080      0.006   14.230    0.000
#>     w1              0.121      0.040    3.063    0.002
#>     w2              0.081      0.045    1.818    0.069
#> 
#> Intercepts:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .f              -0.012      0.009   -1.335    0.182
#> 
#> Covariances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   x1 ~~         
#>     x2              0.110      0.010   11.549    0.000
#>     x3              0.012      0.013    0.896    0.370
#>     w1              0.001      0.004    0.187    0.852
#>     w2              0.001      0.003    0.331    0.740
#>   x2 ~~         
#>     x3              0.099      0.011    8.825    0.000
#>     w1             -0.003      0.002   -1.201    0.230
#>     w2             -0.001      0.003   -0.519    0.604
#>   x3 ~~         
#>     w1             -0.003      0.003   -0.908    0.364
#>     w2              0.003      0.004    0.852    0.394
#>   w1 ~~         
#>     w2             -0.025      0.070   -0.355    0.723
#> 
#> Variances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .f               0.277      0.014   19.539    0.000
#>     x1              1.000                             
#>     x2              1.000                             
#>     x3              1.000                             
#>     w1              1.000                             
#>     w2              1.000                             
#>    .y1              0.115      0.022    5.318    0.000
#>    .y2              0.212      0.017   12.378    0.000
#>    .y3              0.191      0.021    9.165    0.000
#>     f~1             0.601      0.022   27.932    0.000
